stem: Which statement best describes a 'conflict of interest' in the financial services industry?

options.A: A situation in which the objectives of two parties are incompatible or misaligned and a person can use his position to derive personal benefit
options.B: Any situation in which a client loses money on an investment
options.C: A disagreement between a financial institution and the MAS over a regulation
options.D: A situation in which two clients want to buy the same product

explanation: 教材 §1.1 定义利益冲突为两方目标不相容/不一致、某人可借其地位谋取个人利益的情形。B/C/D 均非该定义:亏损、与监管者分歧、客户争购同一产品都不是利益冲突。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §1.1

stem: In a serious case, a conflict of interest violation may be regarded under the law as:

options.B: A purely civil matter with no consequences
options.D: An administrative oversight requiring no action

explanation: 教材 §1.3 指出,某些情形下利益冲突违规在法律上可被视为贪腐罪行(corruption offence),后果远超合约或行政层面。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §1.3

stem: A representative who, while advising a husband and wife, acts in the interest of the husband at the expense of the wife is an example of which type of conflict of interest?

options.A: A 'built-in' conflict that the representative finds himself in
options.B: A conflict that the representative 'creates' through his own actions
options.C: A conflict arising solely from the institution's pricing policy
options.D: A breach of the Personal Data Protection Act

explanation: 教材 §2.3 以此为例说明代表通过自身行为'制造'(created)的冲突。'内建'(built-in)冲突是代表'发现'自己身处的(§2.2),与此不同。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §2.2–2.3

stem: Which of the following is NOT listed in the chapter as one of the manifestations of misaligned interests?
explanation: 教材 §3.3 列出的表现形式为:偏颇建议、强制交叉销售、过度交易、未能执行、披露不足、侵犯隐私。'保证最低投资回报'并不在列。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §3.3

stem: According to the chapter, why is biased advice difficult to eliminate even for well-intentioned representatives?

options.A: Because clients always demand the cheapest product
options.B: Because biases often operate below awareness and incentive/compensation structures are 'built-in' to product pricing
options.C: Because the MAS prohibits disclosure of commissions
options.D: Because retail clients can fully assess product suitability themselves

explanation: 教材 §4.5–4.6 指出偏见常在意识之下运作,且激励与薪酬结构'内建'于产品定价,使代表难以保持无偏。D 与 §4.2 相反(零售客户恰恰难以评估);A、C 无教材依据。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §4.5–4.6

stem: A client is compelled to purchase credit insurance in order to secure a mortgage, without being informed of the need or suitability of that insurance. This best illustrates:
explanation: 教材 §5.4 正以'被迫购买信用保险才能获得按揭'为例说明强制交叉销售的第一种方式:未告知附加产品的需要/适合性即生冲突。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §5.4

stem: A life agent persuades a client to drain an existing policy in order to fund a new policy with the SAME insurance company, solely to generate commission. This practice is known as:
explanation: 教材 §6.7 指出,掏空原保单为'同一家'保险公司的新保单融资、仅为生佣金,属于 churning(经由 switching)。Twisting(§6.8)是转向'另一家'保险公司,故 B 错。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §6.7–6.8

stem: 'De facto discretionary control' over a client's account, relevant to churning, arises when:

options.A: The representative holds a written power of attorney
options.B: The client signs a discretionary management agreement
options.C: The institution sets the commission schedule
options.D: The client is unable to exercise his own judgment and evaluate the representative's recommendations

explanation: 教材 §6.4 指出,当客户无法自行判断、评估代表的建议时,代表被视为对账户拥有'事实上的酌情控制权'。A/B 描述的是正式或合约酌情控制,非'de facto';C 与控制权无关。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §6.4

stem: An agent collects a life insurance premium from a client but simply appropriates the money for his own personal use. Under the chapter, this is characterised as:

options.B: An act of cheating or fraud
options.D: A breach of the suitability standard only

explanation: 教材 §7.6 指出,代理人径直挪用所收保费供己用,该'未能执行'是欺骗或欺诈行为(cheating or fraud),不只是疏忽或延迟。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §7.6

stem: When a representative presents a life insurance illustration as a guarantee of performance, without explaining its hypothetical nature and underlying assumptions, this is an example of:
explanation: 教材 §8.6 指出,演示本是解释保单特性的工具,但若不说明其假设性、甚至当作业绩保证,即产生披露不足/误导的道德问题。§8.7 提及 1995 年 NAIC《人寿保险演示示范规例》。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §8.6–8.7

stem: Which of the following is identified in the chapter as one of the four general duties under the fiduciary standard?

options.A: Duty to maximise the institution's revenue
options.B: Duty to minimise the representative's tax
options.C: Duty of Loyalty — full disclosure of information, including any potential or actual conflict of interest
options.D: Duty to sell at least one product per client meeting

explanation: 教材 §11.3 列出受信标准四义务:谨慎(Care)、忠诚(Loyalty)、服从(Obedience)、诚信(Good Faith)。忠诚义务要求全面披露,包括潜在或实际利益冲突。其余选项均非受信义务。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §11.3

stem: Which statement correctly distinguishes the fiduciary standard from the suitability standard?

options.A: The fiduciary standard is rules-based, while the suitability standard is principles-based
options.B: Both standards require the representative to choose the product paying the highest commission
options.C: The suitability standard requires ongoing monitoring of the product, while the fiduciary standard does not
options.D: The fiduciary standard is principles-based (open-ended), while the suitability standard is rules-based (satisfying applicable rules and notices)

explanation: 教材 §13.3–13.4、§13.11 指出受信标准是原则导向、更开放(基于道德上正确),适合性标准是规则导向、封闭(满足 FAA s.36、FAA-N16 等规则)。A 颠倒;B 与道德义务相悖;C 颠倒(§13.1 指持续监测是受信人的义务)。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §13.1, §13.3–13.4, §13.11

stem: Under FAA Notice No. 16 (FAA-N16), before approving a client's account to trade a LISTED Specified Investment Product (SIP), the financial adviser must conduct a:
explanation: 教材 §13.9 指出,对'非上市'SIP 须作客户知识评估(CKA);对'上市'SIP 须作客户账户审查(Client Account Review),以判断客户是否理解衍生品的风险与特性后方可开通账户交易。故 A 适用于非上市 SIP,B 才是上市 SIP 的要求。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.18 §13.8–13.9
